name: Step 0 → Early Game — Milestone Route
description: Prerequisite route for Step 0 → Early Game, segmented into milestone episodes by the progression-router planner. Each phase closes on a quest/unlock capstone rather than an arbitrary skill number.

steps.detail: Auto-opens on first login after account creation, live inside Tutorial Island's starting house (not a separate pre-game screen). Set body type, skin colour and clothing style, then confirm to enter the house. Every choice here is free to redo later via Makeover once off the island, so a speedrun-efficient line spends zero time deliberating.

media.caption: Tutorial Island start - Gielinor Guide's house (origin:tutorial:01)
Gielinor Guide at the starting house; the tutorial begins here

steps.detail: Inside the starting house. Answer his experience-level question ('brand new' / 'played in the past' / veteran). Answering 'brand new' or 'played in the past' unlocks the Adventure Paths task system, which changes where you spawn in Lumbridge later (see the Lumbridge-arrival step); veteran skips Adventure Paths. Listen through the welcome-to-Gielinor and talk-to-NPCs explanation and his mention of the settings menu. This is the only tutorial dialogue choice with a lasting mainland effect -- both answers complete this step identically, so it is not a fork in the checklist, only in where you land.

hints.note: 'brand new'/'played in the past' unlock the Adventure Paths mainland spawn; veteran does not. Both answers complete the step.

steps.detail: Settings > Display > Graphics. Set camera zoom distance to preference; leave 'Scroll wheel can change zoom distance' enabled for quick manual zoom; optionally set Screen Brightness / Hide roofs. Worth doing now (or during the Guide's settings mention) rather than mid-instruction, since zoom/roofs affect visibility of instructors and objects in Tutorial Island's tightly packed buildings.

Settings > Audio > General. Adjust Master / Sound effect / Music volume sliders to preference (also area music mode, looping, unlock messages, hit sounds). Purely a preference setting -- the wiki lists the sliders but recommends no particular value; no speedrun-specific audio guidance exists.

steps.instruction: Set controls and learn the dialogue-skip techniques
steps.detail: Settings > Controls: toggle Run via the minimap orb, or leave the default Control-key run/walk toggle -- largely moot on the island (short walks, no run-energy pressure) but worth setting once since it carries to the mainland. The two concrete wiki-grounded speedrun techniques for the dialogue-heavy instructors ahead: press Spacebar to auto-select 'Click here to continue', and press a number key to instantly pick the matching numbered dialogue option (mouse-clicking works identically but is slower). Grounding-gap disclosure: no wiki page titled a 'Tutorial Island speedrun guide' exists -- the wiki's only 'Speedrunning' article covers the seasonal Quest Speedrunning minigame, whose own Restrictions section states the tutorial is SKIPPED there entirely (players spawn directly in Lumbridge), so it grounds nothing about in-tutorial pathing/dialogue. These two controls, plus the Skippy NPC full-skip bypass (a lucky, non-reliable trivia -- see the Lumbridge-arrival step), are the only wiki-grounded speedrun-relevant material found for step 0; everything beyond is left '??' rather than invented.

hints.note: Wiki-grounded dialogue-skip techniques (Game_controls.s5) -- the only sourced speedrun material for Tutorial Island besides Skippy.

steps.detail: Exit the starting house through the door and left-click a tile on the ground to walk the path toward the Survival Expert -- click-to-move is the base movement verb from here on; arrow keys pan/rotate the camera (keep the map north-centred so as not to set off in the wrong direction).

Talk to Brynna, the Survival Expert, to receive a small fishing net, then use it on the nearby fishing spot to catch raw shrimps (Fishing xp). Instructions appear top-left; the skills interface shows current level/xp per skill.

steps.instruction: Chop a log and light a fire
steps.detail: Talk to Brynna again to receive a bronze axe and a tinderbox. Use the axe on the nearby tree to collect logs (Woodcutting xp), then use the tinderbox on the logs to light a fire (Firemaking xp). Nearby oak trees are visible but not choppable (need Woodcutting 15, above the level-3 island cap).

Use the raw shrimps on the lit fire to cook them (Cooking xp); eating them afterward restores health but is optional -- health cannot reach 0 on Tutorial Island. Almost every survival skill here caps at level 3 and everything gathered is lost on leaving the island, so a speedrun-efficient line does the single required gather/cook/eat cycle and moves on rather than farming extra shrimps.

steps.instruction: Bake bread with the Master Chef
steps.detail: Enter Building two (the kitchen) and talk to Lev, the Master Chef, to receive a pot of flour and a bucket of water. Use the pot of flour on the bucket of water (or vice versa) to make bread dough, then use the dough on the cooking range to bake bread (Cooking xp). '??' flag: the wiki's own wording on whether Lev replenishes ingredients only after the music tutorial is internally unclear -- not resolved here per own-words/'??' discipline; it doesn't block progression since dough->bread only needs one pass.

steps.instruction: Play a track on the Music Player
steps.detail: Open the Music Player in the kitchen building and select/play a track to complete the prompt. Reads as optional but is a required gate, not a detour -- completing it unlocks emotes and the run-toggle option going forward.

steps.instruction: Talk to the Quest Guide and enter the dungeon
steps.detail: Enter Building three (the quest house) and talk to the Quest Guide, who explains the quest-journal colour code (red = not started, yellow = in progress, green = finished) and the quest-start map icon shown on the minimap/world map. Go down the ladder into the Tutorial Island Dungeon -- shared location for both the Mining Instructor and Combat Instructor ahead.

media.caption: Tutorial Island quest house: the Quest Guide (ori-t-13-talk-to-quest-guide)
Player beside the Quest Guide; he explains the quest-journal colour code before the dungeon ladder

steps.instruction: Mine, smelt and smith a bronze dagger
steps.detail: Talk to Dezzick, the Mining Instructor, to receive a bronze pickaxe. Mine the nearby copper rock and tin rock (Mining xp each), then use the furnace to smelt the copper + tin ore into a bronze bar (Smithing xp). Receive a hammer from Dezzick and use it on the bronze bar at an anvil to smith a bronze dagger (Smithing xp). The wiki calls out pushing both Mining and Smithing to level 3 here as worthwhile -- almost nowhere else has ore, a furnace and an anvil this close together -- but extra reps cost time a speedrun-efficient line doesn't need.

steps.instruction: Kill a giant rat with melee
steps.detail: Talk to Vannaka, the Combat Instructor. Equip the bronze dagger (opens the worn-equipment interface), receive a bronze sword and wooden shield, then open the combat-options interface and pick an attack style (accurate=Attack, aggressive=Strength, defensive=Defence xp; or unarmed punch/kick/block for the same three). Attack and kill one giant rat in the rat pit. Health cannot reach 0 here (floors at 1) so eating is not required; the wiki also documents a 2-damage-then-retreat loop to farm extra melee xp by re-entering combat repeatedly, but that is a deliberate detour, not part of the fastest line. Hitpoints cannot be trained on Tutorial Island.

steps.instruction: Kill a giant rat with Ranged
steps.detail: Talk to Vannaka again to receive a shortbow and 50 bronze arrows. Equip the shortbow and kill one giant rat with Ranged (melee re-entry to the pit is blocked at this point). Talk to Vannaka a third time to close out the combat lesson (combat stats, melee/ranged recap, Wilderness overview).

Enter Building four (the Tutorial Island bank) and use a bank booth to open the bank interface and note the deposit/withdraw mechanics -- no items actually move here. The poll booth is on view but cannot actually be used to vote (it needs a higher total level than obtainable on the island, max 57); Tutorial Island's bank tellers themselves cannot be talked to or traded with, unlike normal bank tellers.

The Account Guide (Financial Advisor) in the bank building gives an Account Management menu overview: membership, worlds/world-switcher, store, bonds, inbox, name changer. Also mentions account safety (bank PIN, authenticator) -- no in-game action, redirects to Customer Support.

Enter Building five (the chapel) and talk to Brother Brace, the Prayer instructor, for the Prayer list, prayer points and recharge explanation. Bury any bones on hand for Prayer xp -- only possible if bones were picked up earlier in the rat pen, otherwise Prayer training is unavailable again until the mainland. The organ upstairs is playable but non-essential -- skip it on a speedrun-efficient line.

media.caption: Tutorial Island chapel: Brother Brace, the Prayer instructor (ori-t-19-talk-to-prayer-instructor)
Player beside Brother Brace; talk to him for the Prayer list and points explanation

steps.detail: Talk to Paul, the Ironman tutor, on the road to the final building, and choose an Ironman mode (Standard / Hardcore / Ultimate / Group) or remain a normal account. Mode is freely switchable while still on the island; after leaving, Adam in Lumbridge only allows downward switches (Ultimate/Hardcore -> Standard -> non-Ironman), and the choice locks permanently after one week. '??': the wiki does not detail the Group Ironman branch's internal steps beyond noting Group Ironman players continue to The Node south of the final building instead of the Magic building.

Enter Building six (the Magic building) and talk to Terrova, the Magic Instructor, to receive 5 air runes and 5 mind runes. Open the spellbook interface, select Wind Strike, and cast it on a nearby chicken (Magic xp). Only one guaranteed cast is allowed here before being told to move on; the wiki notes players can return to the rat pit to push Magic to level 3 (up to 32 of each rune, since xp is granted even on a miss) -- an optional detour, not part of the fastest line.

steps.instruction: Cast Lumbridge Home Teleport to leave the island
steps.detail: Talk to Terrova again. If planning Ironman Mode and it wasn't chosen yet, he asks before you leave -- backtrack to the Ironman tutor first if needed. Cast Lumbridge Home Teleport from the spellbook (auto-taught, no Magic level or rune cost, ~30 min real-time cooldown) to leave Tutorial Island.

hints.note: Only teleport a fresh account has; ~30 min cooldown, free of Magic level/rune cost.

steps.detail: Land in Lumbridge, north-east of Tutorial Island, with standard starting provisions. Spawn point branches on the Gielinor Guide choice (see the character-creation-adjacent dialogue step): outside The Sheared Ram pub near Adventurer Jon -- who immediately introduces Adventure Paths -- if Adventure Paths was unlocked and Ironman Mode was not chosen; inside/near the walled Lumbridge Castle courtyard otherwise. This closes step 0 (Tutorial Island) -- mainland progression is a separate domain. Closing aside, not part of the baseline route: an NPC named Skippy occasionally spawns in the starting house near the Gielinor Guide and can skip the entire tutorial outright, but he isn't permanently present (an anti-bot measure), so this is a lucky bypass rather than a reliable line. Trivia: island-wide caps are max total level 57, max total xp 3334.

steps.instruction: Land in Lumbridge and unlock world chat
steps.detail: Casting Lumbridge Home Teleport off the island permanently unlocks world chat and further skill training. Ironman-mode players are diverted to a separate Ironman tutor before the teleport.

media.caption: Arrive in Lumbridge after Tutorial Island (scenario capture)
Lumbridge arrival scene (scenario capture, oracle PASS)

steps.instruction: Buy a spade at the Lumbridge General Store
steps.detail:
First gear stop: Lumbridge General Store in the castle courtyard -- a non-GE named vendor that restocks on a per-item timer (sellmultiplier=1300/buymultiplier=400/delta=30). Buy a Spade (stock 5) here first -- it's mandatory for X Marks the Spot and not obtainable free anywhere else nearby. Shears (stock 2), Pot (stock 5), Bucket (stock 3) and Tinderbox (stock 2) are also sold here as backups, but Sheep Shearer/Cook's Assistant each hand out or spawn their own copies free during the quest, so only the Spade is a hard buy.

Travel budget for the first hour is walking only: Lumbridge Home Teleport (already used to arrive) resets you back to the castle courtyard, not useful for skipping travel elsewhere since it's the hub you start in. Every other Lumbridge transport option (amulet of glory, gnome glider, fairy ring, ring of returning, canoe) needs an item/quest/level not yet available. The Al Kharid gate costs 10 coins each way until Prince Ali Rescue is done (avoidable via a long detour) -- worth knowing as a coin sink to route around, though none of the four openers ahead require crossing it.

Opener order ahead (own-synthesis from each quest's own Details/startmap + Lumbridge's Quests list -- not from a dedicated speedrun source): Cook's Assistant and Sheep Shearer first, since both start/finish inside or immediately around Lumbridge Castle (kitchen, cellar, cow field, mill, sheep pen, 1st-floor spinning wheel all within ~10-30 tiles) with zero long-distance travel and a shared turn-in visit; The Restless Ghost third, since its only detour is the one-way Wizards' Tower basement trip and the rest (chapel/graveyard/swamp hut) stays Lumbridge-local; X Marks the Spot last, since its dig route is the only opener that pushes travel out to Draynor Village and Falador before finishing at Port Sarim, doubling as the first venture into the wider road network later quests need, with its 200gp + free-skill-lamp reward as a fitting capstone once the cheaper three are banked. Gap flag: no Xerxes/DunkingOreos speedrunner notes exist in this project's wiki-kb study set (those are YouTube creators, not wiki pages, so wikicli-only discipline can't source them) -- this ordering is grounded directly in each quest's own wiki page, not cross-checked against a dedicated speedrun source; flagging so a future agent with those notes can override it.

media.caption: Shop keeper at his stall; buy a spade here first (mandatory for X Marks the Spot)

steps.detail: Lumbridge Cook needs ingredients for the Duke's birthday party. Starter quest, no reqs.

hints.note: start: agree to help with the Duke's birthday cake
Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

media.caption: Lumbridge Castle kitchen: the Cook (quest-cooks-assistant start)
Player beside the Cook; he needs ingredients for the Duke's birthday cake

steps.detail: No requirements. Fred wants 20 balls of wool. Fastest route is simply buying 20 balls of wool (tradeable) and handing them in; the 'intended' route -- shear sheep in the pen with shears, spin the wool at a spinning wheel -- is optional and grants +50 bonus Crafting xp over just buying. Same opener tier as Cook's Assistant, designed to walk new players through Lumbridge.

req_items.name: Balls of wool
req_items.note: unnoted; shears needed instead if shearing/spinning your own

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: Novice quest; only real gate is being able to kill or flee a level 13 skeleton. Talk to Father Aereck about the haunted Lumbridge graveyard, head to Father Urhney's hut west of Lumbridge Swamp and talk to him, then go to the small room in the graveyard, equip the Ghostspeak amulet and talk to the coffin-ghost. Go to the Wizards' Tower basement and search the altar for a ghost's skull (a level 13 skeleton spawns but does not need to be killed, just leave), then use the skull on the open coffin to finish. Required for: Animal Magnetism, Curse of the Empty Lord, Ghosts Ahoy, Making History, Nature Spirit, Creature of Fenkenstrain.

media.caption: Player beside Father Aereck; ask him about the haunted graveyard to start the quest

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

steps.detail: No requirements, tutorial-tier treasure hunt intro quest. Talk to Veos in the Sheared Ram pub, Lumbridge, to start. Dig at 4 marked spots in sequence: by Bob's Brilliant Axes (west of the plant against the wall), behind Lumbridge Castle near the kitchen door, north-west of the Draynor Village jail by the wheat farm, and in the pig pen next to Martin the Master Gardener. Use the resulting ancient casket on Veos at the Port Sarim docks to finish.

req_items.name: Spade
req_items.note: Eastfloor spade does not work for this quest
